response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of the GISCollective open-source GIS platform, Green Map work, FOSS4G speaker) and proven low-latency systems engineering at Sociomantic, where he built asynchronous, high-throughput services in DLang handling millions of requests per second. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ (or Rust), and his professional C++ exposure is brief and dated (roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015), with no Rust at all. His recent work has been web-focused TypeScript/Node.js product engineering, and he has no direct routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline experience, making him a domain-adjacent but technically misaligned fit.
strengths: Founded and maintains an open-source geospatial data platform (GISCollective) used in production by Green Map
Real low-latency distributed-systems experience: async I/O, fibers, and caching in services handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Strong engineering craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship) with Linux, Docker, and Kubernetes operational experience
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and dated (short 2014-2015 stint); no Rust experience to substitute
No experience with routing engines, navigation guidance, or map-tile/routing-data pipelines specifically
Recent years centered on web and full-stack product work (TypeScript, React, MongoDB), far from systems-level data pipeline engineering; an unexplained ~8-month employment gap in 2020
